svg.title: PubChem Molecular Topology
svg.desc: Infographic summarizing 4 PubChem records, with 3 parsed molecules, 1 parse errors, 0 topology errors, an implication map for the topology predicates, and a card for each graph predicate.
svg.text: Graph-theoretic annotations for the current PubChem CID-SMILES release.
PubChem Molecular Topology provides a reproducible workflow for graph-theoretic annotation of molecular structures in PubChem. In the current CID-SMILES
release, 4 records were downloaded and decompressed; 3 were parsed into molecular graphs, 1 failed parsing, and 3 completed topology evaluation after 0 topology
failures in <0.1 s. Among the evaluated graphs, 3 were planar, 3 were outerplanar, 3 were cactus graphs, 3 were bipartite, 0 contained K2,3 homeomorphs, 0
contained K4 homeomorphs, and 0 contained K3,3 homeomorphs. Per-CID boolean annotations were written to Parquet, and connected-component counts plus
exact diameters for connected molecules were stored alongside them, where the diameter is the longest of the shortest paths, while aggregate summaries were
accumulated in the same pass. The source code is available on GitHub, and released result sets are deposited in Zenodo.

g.text: Read dark arrows as direct implications. Dashed red arrows mark forbidden subdivisions for the class above.
Connected and acyclic, with n-1 edges and one simple
A disjoint union of trees: acyclic overall, but not required
Each edge lies on at most one simple cycle, so cycles
can meet only at articulation vertices.
Every cycle of length at least four has a chord, excluding
Can be embedded in the plane with no edge crossings
Planar and drawable with every vertex on the outer face.

g.title: K2,3 homeomorph
g.desc: The complete bipartite graph K2,3 drawn with two vertices in one partition and three in the other.

g.text: Contains a subdivision of K2,3, a classical obstruction to
Contains a subdivision of K3,3, a Kuratowski obstruction
Contains a subdivision of K4, another standard
Vertices split into two partitions, with edges only across
the split and no odd cycle.
